title: Investigate: DC-steering for scarce GPUs + volume-connect phase/% display lag
description:
INVESTIGATION (low priority, spun out of MPI-134). Two separate observations from the 5090 connect session:

=== (A) Any-region vs targeted-DC placement for scarce GPUs ===
Observed 2026-06-25 (logs/app.log L2011-2050): 7 consecutive Pod-create attempts for RTX 5090 with dc=__any__ (vol=none, ephemeral) ALL refused with 'This machine does not have the resources to deploy your pod'. Immediately after, ONE create with dc=EU-RO-1 (vol=jgz607hffi, a hand-made network volume pinned to that DC) succeeded first try (201). The volume itself is incidental -- selecting it FORCED a specific DC; RunPod auto-placement on __any__ kept landing on full hosts. HOWEVER the user later landed a 5090 on __any__ after ~5 MANUAL retries -- so __any__ DOES work with persistence, it's just slower/luckier than targeting a DC that has the card.

QUESTION: For scarce GPUs, should the auto-retry loop steer placement to a specific DC the availability snapshot says has the card (dataCenters[].gpuAvailability[] carries per-DC stockStatus High/Med/Low + available), instead of hammering dc=__any__? Cheapest version: on each retry send the best-stock DC's id instead of null, rather than a full DC sweep. Need to confirm: (1) does ephemeral + specific-DC (no volume) also deploy, or did the volume matter? (2) does the snapshot reliably give per-DC stock for a card showing only on some DCs? (3) is targeted-DC materially faster-to-land than __any__-with-retry, enough to justify the code? If marginal, leave __any__-with-retry (MPI-134) as-is.

KEY SEMANTIC FINDING for the fix: RunPod's per-GPU `available` boolean is OPTIMISTIC -- a card can read available:true with stockStatus 'Low' and still REFUSE the actual createPod ('does not have the resources'). So `available` = catalogue presence, NOT deploy-ability. Both _isPickedGpuInStock (MpiSettings) and _isGpuInStockBoot/_isGpuAvailable (shell.js / remoteProxy.js) gate purely on `available` -- the create attempt is the only ground truth. MPI-134 works around this by treating the refusal as the retry signal; a DC-steering fix should keep doing that, not trust the flag.

=== (B) Volume-backed connect: missing 'connecting' phase + late % ===
Observed same session: a volume-backed 5090 connect showed panel status 'creating' but the hero/status-bar never flipped to the 'connecting' phase the ephemeral path shows; then the % jumped to 32% abruptly and appeared to drop+recover. The Settings connect emits phase:'connecting' once up-front (MpiSettings ~L690) then resolves to null on success; the visible 'connecting · X%' pct comes from the _pollEngineReady / remote:connect-progress events. Hypothesis: volume attach + warm-engine first-poll latency left a gap before the first pct arrived, so the hero looked stuck on 'creating' until 32% appeared. NOT yet log-captured -- needs the connect's phase/pct event sequence from a fresh volume-backed connect to confirm whether it's a real emit-ordering bug or just first-poll latency. Cosmetic; no functional impact (the connect completed).

=== (B-UPDATE 2026-06-25) 'creating' vs 'connecting' lag -- RESOLVED, NOT A BUG ===
Reproduced on a HEALTHY EPHEMERAL pull (no volume, pod yjax3iotxkzw78) -- so it's NOT volume-specific as (B) guessed. Root cause: the panel status text 'creating...' is set immediately on click, while the hero 'CONNECTING - X%' phase+estimate only appears once _pollEngineReady starts emitting remote:connect-progress (poll has create-roundtrip + 4s-interval startup latency). The % itself is a TIME ESTIMATE, not real pull progress: _pct(ms)=clamp(0,99, ms/240000*100) in remoteProxy.js / MpiSettings.js ~L633 -- RunPod's API exposes NO real image-pull % (the console's 'N/13 layers' is not in the API we use). So the app fakes ~4min=100%. The user watched 'creating' -> gap -> 'CONNECTING 45%' and it completed fine. Two surfaces updating at different times + a fake estimate = looks laggy, works correctly. POLISH not bug. Possible polish (optional, low pri): flip panel text to 'connecting' in step with the hero phase, or smooth the % start. Do NOT chase real layer-% -- not in the API.

=== (C) Bad-host detection -- placed-but-undeployable (stuck pull / maintenance) ===
Observed 2026-06-25: TWO bad hosts back to back after landing a 5090. (1) pod ryqcc1a4c4yus2 stuck at 0/13 layers, 0% -- a dead/throttled ghcr.io pull, never progressed. (2) pod t4mzg7jfxhdleu got a RunPod console ALERT badge 'This server is going to be down for routine maintenance and software upgrades' -- placed on a host being drained. In BOTH, the app polls ComfyUI-ready blindly and cannot distinguish 'slow boot' from 'dead host', so the user waits until the ~5min watchdog (watchdogAfterMs=300000) to be nudged to Cancel.
THE SIGNAL IS ALREADY FETCHED AND DISCARDED: remoteProxy.js _podRuntimeStatus (~L421) calls client.getPod(key, podId) and reads ONLY p.desiredStatus -- the rest of the pod object p (which may carry a maintenance/alert flag, machine state, or runtime info) is thrown away. EXITED/'not-running' hosts are already caught (MPI-96) but those are hosts that FAILED TO START; a host stuck PULLING or flagged MAINTENANCE is RUNNING-but-not-ready, a different case not yet handled.
INVESTIGATE: (1) what fields does getPod's p actually contain -- is there a maintenance/alert/banner flag we can read? (2) can we detect a pull stalled at 0% past N seconds (no layer progress)? RunPod's pull-progress may not be in the API -- confirm. (3) surface 'This host looks bad (maintenance/stuck) -- Cancel and retry for a fresh one' BEFORE the 5min watchdog. With MPI-134's retry now working, the loop would auto-grab a fresh host -- but only if we DELETE the bad one fast instead of waiting 5min. Ties directly into the auto-retry UX.

None block MPI-134 (shipped). All are 'nice to understand / improve later'; (C) is the most actionable (concrete signal source, two real instances).
status_note: CLOSED by user 2026-07-02 — headline goal (connect to scarce/enum-rejected cards) SOLVED by MPI-159 (GraphQL create fallback, shipped + live-verified). (B) closed as not-a-bug (fake-% time-estimate, polish only). (A) DC-steer + (C) maintenance-detect SHIPPED (commit b48ac2b) but LOGIC-VERIFIED ONLY — never live-fired (needs a live scarce card + a maintenance host). User accepts A/C as-is; not blocking the close. Stuck-pull-at-0% stays undetectable (no RunPod pull-% API field, confirmed). Files: js/shell.js, routes/remoteProxy.js, MpiSettings.js, docs/gotchas.md.
